vBRIEFInfo.description: Scope vBRIEF ingested from GitHub issue #43

plan.title: Low Temp Warning falsely triggers on rising morning temperatures

narratives.Description: Low Temp Warning falsely triggers on rising morning temperatures
narratives.Overview:
## Bug
The **Low Temp Warning** ("Temps may fall below DD within HH hours") fires in the morning even when the temperature is already **above** the threshold and steadily **rising** (only going up).

## Root cause
The low-temp analysis reads Open-Meteo's `hourly.temperature_2m`. With our `forecast_hours` request parameter, **hourly index 0 is the current (already-elapsed) hour** (verified against the API: `hourly.time[0]` equals `current.time`, rounded down to the hour).

In `OpenMeteoMapper.hoursUntilLowTemp`:
1. The scan starts at **index 0** — the elapsed current hour. Near the dawn minimum, that top-of-hour value can read at/just below the threshold even when the live `current` temperature has already climbed above it.
2. There is **no check on the current temperature**. A "may fall below" alert should only apply when it's currently at/above the threshold and a *future* hour drops below it.

Combined, a warming morning (e.g. 49.5 at the top of the hour → 51 now and rising) trips the warning off the stale index-0 bucket.

The rain warning uses the same index-0 logic but is acceptable there ("rain within ~1 hour" may legitimately include the current hour), so this fix is scoped to the low-temp path only.

## Fix
1. Only emit the low-temp warning when the **current temperature is at/above the threshold**.
2. **Scan future hours only** (skip the elapsed current-hour bucket).
3. Request **one extra forecast hour** so the look-ahead window still covers N full future hours after dropping index 0.
4. Update unit tests + add regressions: "currently above and rising → no warning" and "already below → no warning".
narratives.FunctionalRequirements:
FR-1: The low-temp warning MUST NOT fire when the current temperature is at/above the threshold and no future hour within the window drops below it.
FR-2: The low-temp warning MUST NOT fire when the current temperature is already below the threshold (cold/freeze cues cover that).
FR-3: The analysis MUST consider only future hours, excluding the elapsed current-hour bucket (hourly index 0).
FR-4: The reported hours-until value MUST reflect hours ahead of now (next full hour = 1).
narratives.Architecture: In OpenMeteoMapper: pass the current temperature (converted to Fahrenheit) into hoursUntilLowTemp; guard that current>=threshold else return nil; scan hourly indices 1...clampWindowHours(window) (skip index 0) and return the matching index as hours-ahead. Bump WeatherForecastConfig.forecastHours to clampWindowHours(max(rain,lowTemp))+1 so N future buckets exist after dropping index 0. Leave the rain path (hoursUntilRain) unchanged.
narratives.TestingStrategy: Update existing low-temp mapper tests to the future-only semantics; add regressions for 'current above + rising -> nil' and 'current below threshold -> nil'; update forecastHours expectation (+1). Run xcodebuild test + SwiftLint strict.

items.title: Fix low-temp false trigger (future-only + current-temp guard)

narrative.Acceptance: No warning when above+rising or already below; future-only scan; tests green.

references.title: Issue #43: Low Temp Warning falsely triggers on rising morning temperatures
